summary: Verified P0 fix for POST /api/teaching-load/bulk — semester_id null no longer makes new assignments invisible in active-semester view. All 8 verification steps (Steps 1-8 from the request) pass via 7 new pytest cases. Existing 7-case regression suite in test_teaching_load_semester_backfill.py also still passes (14/14 total).
critical_code_review_comments: teaching_load.py is 2129 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into bulk/, list/, backfill/, conflict/ submodules for maintainability.
GET /teaching-load and bulk endpoints cap reads at 500 docs (.to_list(500)); fine today but a pagination/limit param would be safer as data grows.

Bulk endpoint silently auto-migrates orphan courses to the active semester (a side-effect on the course collection from a teaching-load route). This is intentional per the fix but should be surfaced in the API response (e.g., migrated_courses[]) so the UI can warn the user.
seed_data_creation: Per-test ephemeral courses prefixed 'TLBLK_' + corresponding teaching_loads — fully cleaned up by test_zz_cleanup. Verified DB state post-run is identical to pre-run (15 courses, 9 loads, 0 leftovers).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The bulk endpoint fix is verified end-to-end. If extending: also worth covering (a) a multi-item bulk payload mixing active+old+orphan courses to ensure per-item effective_sem_id resolution is independent, and (b) a non-admin user (dept_head) hitting bulk to verify _ensure_course_in_user_scope still rejects out-of-scope courses (admin bypasses scope). Tests are isolated and re-runnable safely.
rca of the issue: Frontend (teaching-load.tsx handleSave) sends {teacher_id, course_id, weekly_hours} only — semester_id is omitted. Previous bulk handler inserted teaching_load with semester_id=null. GET /api/teaching-load defaults to {semester_id: active}, so null-semester loads were invisible until the next Cloud Run cold-start ran the backfill (~1h). Fix: bulk now resolves effective_sem_id = item.semester_id || course.semester_id || active_semester_id, and auto-promotes orphan courses to the active semester (so backfill won't revert). Strictness validated: courses already pinned to an OLD/closed semester are NOT pulled into active (Step 5).
